ddls:
CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state */,
  "state_abbreviation" TEXT /* Abbreviation of the state */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of the state */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the state */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment_stats" (
  "employment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment record */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the state */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the employment data */,
  "quarter" INTEGER /* Quarter of the employment data */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the employment data */,
  "employment_type" TEXT /* Type of employment */,
  "employment_count" INTEGER /* Total employment count */,
  "employment_per_thousand" REAL /* Employment per thousand jobs */,
  "location_quotient" REAL /* Location quotient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_stats_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wage_stats" (
  "wage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wage record */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the state */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the wage data */,
  "quarter" INTEGER /* Quarter of the wage data */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the wage data */,
  "wage_type" TEXT /* Type of wage */,
  "hourly_mean_wage" REAL /* Hourly mean wage */,
  "annual_mean_wage" REAL /* Annual mean wage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_wage_stats_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator */,
  "state_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the state */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the economic indicator */,
  "sub_category" TEXT /* Sub-category of the economic indicator */,
  "indicator_name" TEXT /* Name of the economic indicator */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the economic indicator */,
  "date_reported" TEXT /* Date the indicator was reported */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the economic indicator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_state_id FOREIGN KEY ("state_id") REFERENCES states ("state_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., economist, researcher, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who generated the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "generation_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Parameters used to generate the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report */,
  "data_range" TEXT /* Data range of the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data provided by the source */,
  "data_update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data updates */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Last update date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_exports" (
  "export_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data export */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who exported the data */,
  "export_date" TEXT /* Date the data was exported */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data exported */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the exported data */,
  "export_format" TEXT /* Format of the exported data */,
  "export_size" INTEGER /* Size of the exported data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("export_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_exports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API access */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who accessed the API */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date the API was accessed */,
  "endpoint" TEXT /* API endpoint accessed */,
  "response_code" INTEGER /* HTTP response code */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* API key used for access */,
  "request_body" TEXT /* Request body of the API call */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
